file_insights.purpose: Auto-Claude spec tracker and status metadata
file_insights.changes: Updated subtasks 4-1 and 4-2 from 'pending' to 'completed' status; added completion notes and ISO timestamps (2026-03-07T04:22:42 and 2026-03-07T04:25:00)
file_insights.role: Spec orchestration document - maintains canonical status of all subtasks

file_insights.purpose: Comprehensive technical documentation of all spec 017 changes and implementation details
file_insights.changes: Created 263-line markdown document replacing stub result from spec 016; documents 4 implementation phases with detailed technical breakdown
file_insights.role: Acceptance criteria verification artifact and technical reference

patterns_discovered.description: Result.md organizes changes into 4 logical phases: (1) Dispatcher changes, (2) Zellij button, (3) Log tail view, (4) Integration & build. Each phase has file modifications, verification steps, and commit references.
patterns_discovered.significance: Enables clear traceability from implementation to documentation

patterns_discovered.description: Each technical change section includes explicit verification checkmarks (✅) with build/test results inline. Testing Notes section provides structured table of 355 tests passing with zero failures.
patterns_discovered.significance: Documentation doubles as proof of completion; stakeholders can verify claims in result.md

patterns_discovered.description: All 5 acceptance criteria from spec appear explicitly in final Acceptance Criteria Status table with completion status and implementation notes
patterns_discovered.significance: Creates verifiable link between spec requirements and deliverable evidence

patterns_discovered.description: Result.md includes data flow diagram (ASCII art), key files modified table, and known limitations section with concrete reasons (e.g., 'sandbox restrictions' for timer-based refresh)
patterns_discovered.significance: Provides context for future maintainers on architectural constraints and trade-offs

gotchas_discovered.detail: Timer-based 2-second polling instead of real-time file watching because Quick Look extensions run in strict sandbox with no FSEvents access
gotchas_discovered.impact: Log display has inherent 2-second lag; documented explicitly to manage user expectations

gotchas_discovered.detail: ZellijWebButton only works if Zellij's HTTP server is running (default on 0.43+ but not guaranteed). Link fails silently if server is disabled.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Users may see button but clicking it does nothing if Zellij web is misconfigured

gotchas_discovered.detail: Implementation assumes `logs/stdout.log` and `logs/stderr.log` exist as specific filenames. Original spec may have suggested different naming convention (e.g., `stdout` without `.log` extension).
gotchas_discovered.impact: LiveLogView will not display logs if naming convention differs; could cause silent feature failure

gotchas_discovered.detail: LiveLogView displays exactly last 30 lines from log files; this value is hard-coded in implementation
gotchas_discovered.impact: No user configurability; may be insufficient for verbose logs or unnecessary for quiet ones

discoveries.approach_outcome: SUCCESS - Documentation artifact successfully created with comprehensive technical coverage

approach_details.task_objective: Create output/result.md documenting all changes from spec 017 implementation
approach_details.execution: Wrote 263-line markdown document with 4-phase structure, explicit verification checkmarks, testing table (355/355 tests), acceptance criteria status table, and architectural diagrams
approach_details.completion: All acceptance criteria met: (1) dispatcher zellij_session change documented, (2) ZellijWebButton component documented, (3) LiveLogView with auto-refresh documented, (4) xcodebuild success verified, (5) result.md exists
quality_gates_passed: All 355 tests passed (323 unit + 10 dispatcher harness + 17 job control harness + 4 merge gate harness + 1 serve smoke)
xcodebuild succeeded with clean code signing
No clippy warnings or formatting issues

recommendations.rationale: 30-line hard limit may be insufficient for detailed debugging or excessive for simple cards. Allow users to configure line count (e.g., 10, 30, 50, 100 lines) or implement dynamic height based on window size.

recommendations.item: Add Zellij web server availability check
recommendations.rationale: ZellijWebButton fails silently if Zellij web server is disabled. Button could show disabled state or tooltip explaining requirement.
recommendations.effort: Medium - requires health check to `http://127.0.0.1:8082/` on load

recommendations.item: Document Zellij version requirement in user-facing materials
recommendations.rationale: Result.md notes 'Zellij 0.43+' dependency but users may not consult this file. Add requirement to main README or Quick Look extension documentation.

recommendations.item: Implement ANSI color code parsing for syntax-highlighted logs
recommendations.rationale: Current monospace display strips colors; full logs may contain ANSI escape codes. SwiftUI has no built-in ANSI parser but third-party packages exist.
recommendations.effort: High - requires ANSI parser library and SwiftUI rendering update

recommendations.item: Consider fallback UI when Zellij web server is unavailable
recommendations.rationale: Instead of silent failure, could offer alternative action (e.g., 'Copy pane ID to clipboard' or 'Attach to pane directly')
recommendations.effort: Medium - requires health check + conditional UI
